index.title: learn coding, find community
index.subtitle: Queer Coding Camp, 4. - 10. October 2026, Sumiswald, Bern
index.illustration: Pixel-Art Illustration that shows a group of queer people programming together in a cozy cabin.

supporters.title: Supported by
supporters.text: Queer Coding Camp is made possible with the financial support of foundations and sponsorship contributions from companies. Would you also like to support the project? Find out how in our SME sponsorship overview.

faq.who: Who can attend Queer Coding Camp?
faq.who-text: The camp is open to all queer and questioning people aged 16 to 23. FINTA (Women, Inter, Non-Binary, Trans and Agender People) and PoC (Persons of Color) are strongly underrepresented in the tech industry and thus have priority in the registration process.
faq.cost: What does the Queer Coding Camp cost?
faq.cost-text: Thanks to the support of foundations and private donations, participation is free of charge. You have to pay for your own travel. In the run-up to the camp we organize travel groups from Zurich and Bern. If that is a hurdle for you, you can note this when registering and we will find a solution.
faq.skills: Do I need prior knowledge or a laptop?
faq.skills-text: You don't need any programming knowledge or affinity for IT. If you have your own laptop, please bring it with you. If not, we will provide one for you.
faq.when: Where and when can I sign up?
faq.when-text: We open the registration for the camp in spring. You can subscribe to our newsletter, so you'll be notified as soon as registration opens.
faq.accessibility-text: The Schlafschloss Sumiswald used to be a nursing home, which is why it's accesible for people using wheelchairs. Get in touch with us, to discuss your needs.
faq.language: What language skills do I need?
faq.language-text: Core courses (Web and Game) have so far been held in German while workshops and guest lectures sometimes were conducted in English. All instructors are fluent in German and English. Participants who speak either language should be able to follow the course.

info.title: Info
info.coding-camp-text: Queer Coding Camp is a one-week camp where you can gain your first experience with programming. Queer programmers share their skills and help you with your own projects.
info.coding-camp-text-2: You'll learn basic knowledge in web or game development. In addition, there are workshops and inputs on topics such as design and artificial intelligence.
info.coding-camp-text-3: In the evenings there are games, pen and paper or get togethers at the campfire.
info.location-text: The camp takes place at the Schlafschloss Sumiswald, in Emmental (Canton of Bern). As a participant, you stay in two- or three-bed rooms.

about.title: About us
about.text-1: The tech industry can feel exclusionary to people who are not cis, male, white and heterosexual. This was not always the case and needs to change again.
about.text-2: Queer Coding Camp aims to counteract this situation through education and support of young queer people and by providing opportunities for networking of LGBTQ+ people in the tech industry. We queer the tech world, to make it more inclusive, fair and safe for everyone.
about.participate-text-1: We are always looking for tutors or mentors who want to teach at the camp. Contact us at hi@queercodingcamp.ch if you're interested.
about.participate-text-2: You can also become a member of the Queer Coding Camp association. You'll receive an invitation to our members' meeting, where you can give inputs on association activities and future projects.
about.eva: Eva is a neuroscientist studying the hypothalamus, an academic mentor and peer counsellor, and giant trans person.
about.liam: Liam is an interdisciplinary designer and writer. His design work, which ranges from independent apps to global platforms, informs his writing and teaching on the theory, meaning, and positionality of the software interface.
about.olivier: Olivier is a product developer at the online magazine Republik. Before that, he co-founded the climate startup Electricity Maps in Copenhagen. In autumn 2021 he initiated the Queer Coding Camp project.
about.skyler: Skyler is training to become a corporate mentor to support people in learning, change, and development processes. In addition to their work as a development engineer, Skyler has been involved in DEI and awareness teams. They participated in the QCC in 2022.
about.beau: Beau is the founder of the game studio Stardust and has been a game designer and programmer for over ten years.

signup.title: Sign up
signup.newsletter-text: Stay informed, when the registration opens: Sign up for our newsletter.
signup.text: Register now for Queer Coding Camp from 4th to 10th October in Sumiswald. Registration deadline: Monday, 30th June at 23:59. Participation is free of charge.
signup.text-closed: The registration for the 2026 edition of Queer Coding Camp will open in June 2026.

donate.title: Help us queer the tech world!
donate.text-1: With your donation you enable young queer people to participate in the Queer Coding Camp.
donate.text-2: The Queer Coding Camp association was recognized as a non-profit association by the canton of Zurich in 2024 and is therefore tax-exempt. Contact us for donation certificates (for donations of CHF 100 or more per year).
donate.text-3: Donations can also be made via bank transfer:

Location: 8003 Zurich

IBAN: CH25 0900 0000 1584 8382 5

index.title: Queer Coding Camp - Learn Programming for LGBTQ+ Youth in Switzerland
index.description: Learn programming at Queer Coding Camp! Free week-long web & game development course for queer people aged 16-23.

about.title: About Us - Queer Coding Camp Team & Mission for Tech Diversity
about.description: Meet the Queer Coding Camp team and learn about our mission to make the tech industry more inclusive for LGBTQ+ people.

signup.title: Sign Up - Queer Coding Camp 2025 | Free Programming Course
signup.description: Register now for Queer Coding Camp 2025! Free programming week for queer youth in Sumiswald, Switzerland. Limited spots available.

donate.title: Donate - Support Queer Coding Camp
donate.description: Help us make tech more diverse and enable young LGBTQ+ people to learn to code. Donate to the non-profit Queer Coding Camp.

sme.seo-description: Sponsor from CHF 1,000: visibility on our website and in marketing materials. Support young queer people entering tech. Budget, timeline, and contacts.
sme.title: Queer Coding Camp 2026 – SME sponsorship
sme.summary: We are looking for IT companies to support the Queer Coding Camp youth camp with at least CHF 1,000 – and in doing so, help young queer people find their place in the tech world. In return, sponsors are prominently featured and linked on our website and in future promotional materials.
sme.unified-1: For many queer people, the tech industry still doesn't feel like a place for them. Those who don't fit the default mould rarely feel welcomed, and many talented people are lost before they ever get a foothold. Queer Coding Camp addresses this directly: we bring young LGBTQ+ people together, give them space to take their first steps in programming, and connect them with queer professionals from the industry.
sme.unified-2: The one-week youth camp takes place in 2026 from 4–10 October at Schlafschloss Sumiswald. 16 participants aged 16 to 21 learn the basics of web and game development, guided by queer programmers as instructors and mentors. The camp is organised by the non-profit Queer Coding Camp association (founded 2021).
sme.finance-1: In 2025 the camp ran for the fourth time thanks to LGBTI Youth Fund, Pro Helvetia, Hasler Foundation, and SME sponsors. For 2026 we still welcome support from the IT sector.
